codeReview.summary: The feature correctly narrows the public ForeignPersistence protocol by removing create_at, preserves explicit-id flows through exists_record + ensure_at, and leaves lookup-driven creation on create with no correctness issues found.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker could not load the rust-library-worker skill from the path implied by the review instructions because the skill file lives under the repo-local .factory tree, not the user-level .factory tree the generic review prompt suggests.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Review task required reading .factory/skills/<skillName>/SKILL.md; C:\Users\admin\.factory\skills\rust-library-worker\SKILL.md was missing, while the worker transcript for session bbca4d1c-3ceb-47fc-b160-1e0392b90078 shows the actual workflow relied on repo-local guidance and completed successfully.

summary: Reviewed handoff, commit 9f3ec69, transcript skeleton, mission guidance, and relevant source/tests. The narrowed ForeignPersistence surface satisfies VAL-G-PROTOCOL-001/002/003, and no blocking or non-blocking code defects were identified.
